title: [lora_l2.c:507-509] lora_data.eui64 written without zeroing first
description:
Gap: generate_eui64 writes directly to lora_data.eui64 on success, but on failure the buffer may contain partial/stale data.

What: Lines 507-509 set lora_data fields. Line 517 calls generate_eui64(lora_data.eui64). On failure, generate_eui64 zeros its output (line 325), but lora_data.eui64 is never explicitly pre-zeroed before the call.

Why it matters: If generate_eui64 is modified to remove its cleanup code, or if lora_data.eui64 was partially written by a previous failed init attempt (since init is idempotent), stale EUI-64 data could persist. While current code is safe due to generate_eui64's cleanup, the defensive approach would be to zero lora_data.eui64 before the call.
close_reason: Already handled: generate_eui64() at line 357 explicitly zeros the eui64 output buffer on any failure path (secure_zero(eui64, 8) when ret != 0). The defensive zeroing requested in this issue is already implemented.
